["Rebranded zte with leather slapped onto it. Doesnt worth at this price", "Anonymous, 27 minutes agoOne year old chip for 6k. What a joke. you can build a whole computer that is miles better than whatever that is with that price", "One year old chip for 6k. What a joke.", "Skoda9635, 59 minutes agoHard to say what is worse, Honor Purse or this. Well Purse came from Honor, so probably it got... moreat least with the purse there's an actual gimmick attached to it, and i actually liked it compared to the Vertu BetaFold", "VYNI - LosEstupido, 1 hour agoi love when phones are treated like fashion statements more than actual utility devices with f... moreI agree with absolutely everything you said, the reason I like (the concept) though is because it's so bad that it's actually good LOL (I don't support vertu and think they're terrible btw)", "VYNI - LosEstupido, 1 hour agoi love when phones are treated like fashion statements more than actual utility devices with f... moreHard to say what is worse, Honor Purse or this. Well Purse came from Honor, so probably it got some software updates and customer service and maybe QA was better.", "Stupid Chinese company that bought the OG Vertu brand. Faking ads with AI, faking luxury by using Nubia phones. Pure scam.", "WitheredSoul, 1 hour agoBuns company, buns phone, absurd, absolutely atrocious price.

Love it fr, keep it coming Ve... morei love when phones are treated like fashion statements more than actual utility devices with fun gimmicks on the side. this company has to be the worst out of all companies i've seen, genuinely why would someone need a Vertu? do they wanna use it as a utility product? literally any phone with a Snapdragon 8 Elite SoC and even iPhones are miles cheaper than it. do they want it as a fashion statement? iPhone Pros exist, you'd probably have a better experience with them than whatever outlandish UX this company makes. 
 
also love the AI images they used for the ads, keep it going vertu: the super duper ultra premium brand, the alpha and the omega.", "I hope the Enigma (Zack JRE) tests it after getting it from another Alpha (MKBHD). will it prove itself to be a true Alpha, or will it be an Omega pretending to be an Alpha...", "Nubia Fold Vertu edition", "Buns company, buns phone, absurd, absolutely atrocious price. 
 
Love it fr, keep it coming Vertu, I like seeing outlandish products"]

["-, 28 May 20261. How did you get banned in under a minute ???
2. Showed up in certifications, IMEI database... moreOh, okay. I guess we will have to wait and see what they come up with. If it's anything like the Asha models, that will be nice. I hope it comes with a usable OS though, not S30+ or RTOS Touch or whatever it is called. 
 
And I didn't get banned or anything. I just don't have an account anymore. I post without signing up - rarely only now. People argue too much on here, so I keep my distance. :)", "Missing stuff that would actually make it viable. This is e waste", "Dead on arrival.", "AnonD-1231567, 28 May 2026Huh? Where’d you hear this news? If it’s true, I’ll get one for sure! 🥳1. How did you get banned in under a minute ??? 
2. Showed up in certifications, IMEI database and on a page on HMD's website for SAR value information. Idk what it's like but it will probably be HMD's flagship product for the year, headlining the AI feature set on feature phones which was first introduced on Chinese HMD 101 and 102 4G models last year", "The HMD Grade looks promising — if only the rounded corners on this phone matched the precision of the camera module, it would be more balanced.✨", "-, 28 May 2026By the way if anyone's wondering I don't think HMD is Actually working on any new sm... moreHuh? Where’d you hear this news? If it’s true, I’ll get one for sure! 🥳", "Pumpino, 28 May 2026E-waste. At least HMD is no longer dragging Nokia's name through the mud. I wonder if we&... moreI'm neither excited nor disappointed, knowing that none of the devices shared by that user gets actually released.", "Anonymous, 28 May 20266,000mAh battery vs samsung's 5,000mAh = ✔️
6GB RAM (base) vs samsung's 4GB RAM (ba... moredon't. HMD deliver quarterly update across all devices these days including my own Skyline. The real concern is their frequency to update the phone in case there's a critical bug, and I lose LE Audio when HMD updated this phone to android 15 and never enabled it back. While I don't like Samsung, that A17 is better in the long run or get CMF phones if you prefer near stock and few bloat", "4g, wifi 5, bezels thick as phones from 10 years ago, 33w charging, 90hz lcd, poor soc. 
 
This phone is a budget phone from 5 years ago.", "It's vivo Y16 from 2022", "DarlingYext, 28 May 2026Looks like a generic Oppo A6 model, and is just as much of an e-waste. HMD should focus on the... moreHmd luna as same as Oppo A6", "50MP selfies oh great like the Pulse Pro and the MIA Pulse 2 Pro née the actually launched M-Kopa X30.", "The processor is snapdragon 6s gen2 4G", "By the way if anyone's wondering I don't think HMD is Actually working on any new smartphones BUT! They have the TA-1779 aka Asha 305 coming up. Yes I'm serious", "Like a Redmi 15c without the nipple notch same size and style. 
 
Who wants a 6.9" brick  
 
Why can they not go about looking at a successor to the skyline  
 
Oh and the 2 OS updates I would take with a rather large pinch of salt as they fall back on their promises all the time. 
 
I believe they still have that gargling audio bug in video camera on the Pulse models they never bothered to fix.", "At least it's not HD+ display. Hope those crap finally get btfo'd out of existence.", "6,000mAh battery vs samsung's 5,000mAh = ✔️ 
6GB RAM (base) vs samsung's 4GB RAM (base) = ✔️ 
3 years support (OS and security) vs samsung's 6 years support (OS and security) = ❌ 
and a 3.5mm headphone jack = ✔️ 
SD card slot = ❓ 
 
Definitely better than A17, and I am 75% considering this phone. 
If the price is around $100 ~ $110 USD (€85 ~ €95 EUR), then I'm sold! 
 
Keep in mind that samsung is raising the prices of their phone due to the worker strike..... I mean, the artifical RAM shortage caused by the RAM triopoly cartel (samsung, s k hynix, and micron). 
But definitely not greed, oh no. Not greed at all. 
https://www.gsmarena.com/samsung_is_forced_to_raise_prices_due_to_everrising_ram_prices-news-72997.php", "Looks like a generic Oppo A6 model, and is just as much of an e-waste. HMD should focus on the Skyline series, and stop releasing any other nonsensical phone, like this one.", "E-waste. At least HMD is no longer dragging Nokia's name through the mud. I wonder if we'll see the Skyline 2 at some stage.", "Snapdragon 6s Gen 2 is rebranded or overclocked version of Snapdragon 685, no thanks HMD"]

column7:
["pl2rts, 7 hours agoYou get a really good middle class phone for the money that they ask while ripping off Europea... moreI've personally found Oura to be the most accurate tracking when sleeping for me, better than other devices I've tried.  I also dislike wearing a watch/band overnight.  I'm in the US though, so prices are lower than the EU. I can easily afford it and for me, health monitoring is worth it as I love data.", "pl2rts, 7 hours agoYou get a really good middle class phone for the money that they ask while ripping off Europea... moreTech companies outside Europe are overpricing their products in Europe. 
 
Chinese flagship phone could cost almost half the price in China but in Europe even if we add 25 percent vat phone could be priced almost the double here. 
 
Some United States tech companies does this too.", "You get a really good middle class phone for the money that they ask while ripping off Europeans with a lot higher price. 399 dollars is around 342 euro and they ask 429 for it that is like 499 dollars.  
 
While the color is different they ask 100 dollars or 100 euro for the color only is even more insane.  
 
Ahh well in the end of the day the people who buy this deserve to be ripped off if they are willing to spend so much on so small thing with just few censors built in it"]

Ahh well in the end of the day the people who buy this deserve to be ripped off if they are willing to spend so much on so small thing with just few censors built in it"]

["I have jbl go 3. Good small speaker. Battery life is only 5 hours. But build quality is good. And bass is good too. Though I use it very rarely. Mostly listen to music in my car and sometimes on tws earbuds.", "The Xiaomi sound outdoor cost about the same, but way better than this.", "The TWS pair support definitely put it on my list.", "Anonymous, 5 hours agoThe bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreWith a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle. 
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound Outdoor that are 30W and in stereo mode, what a pleasure to use ! I rarely use them inside, but for outside, it's more pleasant than a Charge 6 (not better than 2 Charge 6 though) because of the separation/rendition. 
For the price point, no app (meaning no EQ but also no or less tracking) but a banger, paid 60 € for both, so the price of a Flip 6 in huge promotion ... Better deal, Scamsung can suck it, specially when you see the Xiaomi Sound Party with Harman tuning that seems better than a Charge 6 (I didn't tested the Sound Party to have a honnest opinion about it)", "The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're getting a safe, crowd-pleasing tuning that's suited for most styles of music. 
 
So, weak bass then. 😁", "Yes people really need another "ATLAS" 
Annoying Teenager Loud Audio System.", "looks like a jbl grip level speaker with 30% lower price. good bargain."]

Still better than the smaller iPhone sensor.", "Its better to give an autofocus selfie camera first on your series dear oppo, because x9 basic is not getting autofocus", "Coolingen, 30 May 2026Sometimes you want other format on the back cameras compared to the frontYou can choose that in the settings", "Can't wait to see 300MP 1/4" cameras with all these marketing bs", "Anonymous, 30 May 2026Have you ever heard of pixel binning? And besides, 1/1.8" is the largest sensor ever used... moreThere's this big misconception that pixel binning automatically overcomes the limitations of having tiny pixels. When it comes to cameras and the physics of light, good in=good out. Pixel binning isn't going to magically compensate for the fact that the pixels you're binning are ridiculously small and can't gather enough light to be worth binning anyway. column7:
["I have jbl go 3. Good small speaker. Battery life is only 5 hours. But build quality is good. And bass is good too. Though I use it very rarely. Mostly listen to music in my car and sometimes on tws earbuds.", "The Xiaomi sound outdoor cost about the same, but way better than this.", "The TWS pair support definitely put it on my list.", "Anonymous, 5 hours agoThe bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreWith a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle. 
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound Outdoor that are 30W and in stereo mode, what a pleasure to use ! I rarely use them inside, but for outside, it's more pleasant than a Charge 6 (not better than 2 Charge 6 though) because of the separation/rendition. 
For the price point, no app (meaning no EQ but also no or less tracking) but a banger, paid 60 € for both, so the price of a Flip 6 in huge promotion ... Better deal, Scamsung can suck it, specially when you see the Xiaomi Sound Party with Harman tuning that seems better than a Charge 6 (I didn't tested the Sound Party to have a honnest opinion about it)", "The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're getting a safe, crowd-pleasing tuning that's suited for most styles of music. 
 
So, weak bass then. 😁", "Yes people really need another "ATLAS" 
Annoying Teenager Loud Audio System.", "looks like a jbl grip level speaker with 30% lower price. good bargain."]

For the price point, no app (meaning no EQ but also no or less tracking) but a banger, paid 60 € for both, so the price of a Flip 6 in huge promotion ... Better deal, Scamsung can suck it, specially when you see the Xiaomi Sound Party with Harman tuning that seems better than a Charge 6 (I didn't tested the Sound Party to have a honnest opinion about it)", "The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're getting a safe, crowd-pleasing tuning that's suited for most styles of music. 
 
So, weak bass then. 😁", "Yes people really need another "ATLAS" 
Annoying Teenager Loud Audio System.", "looks like a jbl grip level speaker with 30% lower price. good bargain."]

There's already plenty of phones with 200MP cameras, more than enough to implement this.", "Chinese copying iPhones square selfie sensor is, at least, more useful than names, designs and Liquid Chinese Glass knockoffs. 
But like most things Chinese brands copies, they’ll make it worse: 100MP on a tiny 1/2.5" sensor 💀", "Square Sensor + Horizon Lock stabilization = Perfection", "CamoGeko-XDA, 30 May 2026Yep, this is one of the reasons the first pixel phones were excellent. 👍🏻Even if they had snapdragon their design, battery siize and charging speeds were crap.", "Zuzuz, 30 May 2026There's a problem with the sensor suppliers. I would like to see produced an 1" type sensor in which the size of the pixel is above 2 or 3ym, like in real cameras. Not long ago people had cameras with 5mp, 8mp, 12mp, 16mp, and the quality was and still is excellent.", "A 1/2.5 Sensor for 100MP sounds like an 500kw car transfering it's power to 3 inch wheels.  
 
 
Lots of noise for nothing."]

column7:
["I have jbl go 3. Good small speaker. Battery life is only 5 hours. But build quality is good. And bass is good too. Though I use it very rarely. Mostly listen to music in my car and sometimes on tws earbuds.", "The Xiaomi sound outdoor cost about the same, but way better than this.", "The TWS pair support definitely put it on my list.", "Anonymous, 5 hours agoThe bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreWith a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle. 
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound Outdoor that are 30W and in stereo mode, what a pleasure to use ! I rarely use them inside, but for outside, it's more pleasant than a Charge 6 (not better than 2 Charge 6 though) because of the separation/rendition. 
For the price point, no app (meaning no EQ but also no or less tracking) but a banger, paid 60 € for both, so the price of a Flip 6 in huge promotion ... Better deal, Scamsung can suck it, specially when you see the Xiaomi Sound Party with Harman tuning that seems better than a Charge 6 (I didn't tested the Sound Party to have a honnest opinion about it)", "The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're getting a safe, crowd-pleasing tuning that's suited for most styles of music. 
 
So, weak bass then. 😁", "Yes people really need another "ATLAS" 
Annoying Teenager Loud Audio System.", "looks like a jbl grip level speaker with 30% lower price. good bargain."]

["wthf.. 100mb selfie.. no point, useless, who need so big selfie cam, its just eat battery and main camera goodies...i think 12mb selfie is enough to get pic you and your frends side by side...another else waiste.", "This will probably be a quad bayer 25mp sensor. Still better than the smaller iPhone sensor.", "Its better to give an autofocus selfie camera first on your series dear oppo, because x9 basic is not getting autofocus", "Coolingen, 30 May 2026Sometimes you want other format on the back cameras compared to the frontYou can choose that in the settings", "Can't wait to see 300MP 1/4" cameras with all these marketing bs", "Anonymous, 30 May 2026Have you ever heard of pixel binning? And besides, 1/1.8" is the largest sensor ever used... moreThere's this big misconception that pixel binning automatically overcomes the limitations of having tiny pixels. When it comes to cameras and the physics of light, good in=good out. Pixel binning isn't going to magically compensate for the fact that the pixels you're binning are ridiculously small and can't gather enough light to be worth binning anyway. There's a reason why most real cameras don't do this sort of thing while smartphones do", "ProMaxUltraAreBricks, 30 May 2026That's for the Honor one, Oppo sensor is still 'said to measure 1/2.5 inches in size... moreThe Article says 1/2.5 100MP Sensor for the Oppo phone  and another 100MP Sensor but 1/1.8 in size for the Honor phone.", "Would be hilarious if after all this upgrade, the Magic9 front facing camera won't have AF just like the Magic8 Pro Air.", "Anonymous, 30 May 2026Did you even read the article?
When Chinese copy they make it better.

This 100Mp is 1/1.8&... moreIf you check the cost of sensors used on all flasghips, the combined 3 sensors on the top end iPhone are the cheapest ones. 
Only the Ultra flagships From Chinese brands actually spend on bigger more expensive sensors. 
Yet another instance of low class experience provided by Apple.", "Good that, they focus on selfie camera as well. However, i do not see a benefit of using 100mp instead of 24mp or even 12mp. High mp count on rear cameras enable better digital zoom quality.", "sounds great, in full support of this 
 
just hope they dont pull an apple/google by having such good hardware that all the photos come out overprocessed cus they're using older algorithms. 
 
crazy that we might be getting selfie cams that are bigger than uw cams, seems quite odd but well i'd rather have this.", "ProMaxUltraAreBricks, 30 May 2026That's for the Honor one, Oppo sensor is still 'said to measure 1/2.5 inches in size... moreHave you ever heard of pixel binning? And besides, 1/1.8" is the largest sensor ever used for a selfie camera outside of the flip-out cameras on the old Asus Zenfones and the foldable phones where you can use the rear cameras to take selfies.", "Anonymous, 30 May 2026Did you even read the article?
When Chinese copy they make it better.

This 100Mp is 1/1.8&... moreThat's for the Honor one, Oppo sensor is still 'said to measure 1/2.5 inches in size' (quote from the article), DiD YoU EvEn ReAd ThE ArTiCle?? 🤡 
 
And even 1/1.8" it's still too tiny for 100MP anyway.", "Had x9 pro, terrible phone, every photo cripled by AI. Washed high iso unnatural crap.", "ProMaxUltraAreBricks, 30 May 2026Chinese copying iPhones square selfie sensor is, at least, more useful than names, designs and... moreWhat will you say when Apple copies the rear display from Xiaomi?", "Anonymous, 30 May 2026Did you even read the article?
When Chinese copy they make it better.

This 100Mp is 1/1.8&... morePeople who don't even know which iPhone model they have are going to think iPhone has the best camera.", "Zuzuz, 30 May 2026There's a problem with the sensor suppliers. Even if you want 12 huge megapixels/photodio... moreTechnically those "50 Mpix" sensors are 12.5Mpix sensors with a single-exposure HDR. 
Problem is manufacturers crank the sharpness and denoising wayy to much in post-processing.", "ProMaxUltraAreBricks, 30 May 2026Chinese copying iPhones square selfie sensor is, at least, more useful than names, designs and... moreDid you even read the article? 
When Chinese copy they make it better. 
 
This 100Mp is 1/1.8" in size, much larger and an expensive sensor than the tiny cheap secondary cameras on IPhone's top model.", "AngryLithuanian, 30 May 2026If it's such a big deal to rotate the phone while taking selfies why not include the same... moreSometimes you want other format on the back cameras compared to the front", "marketing only to promote the "square" sensor like the iPhone", "If it's such a big deal to rotate the phone while taking selfies why not include the same tech for rear cameras? There's already plenty of phones with 200MP cameras, more than enough to implement this.", "Chinese copying iPhones square selfie sensor is, at least, more useful than names, designs and Liquid Chinese Glass knockoffs. 
But like most things Chinese brands copies, they’ll make it worse: 100MP on a tiny 1/2.5" sensor 💀", "Square Sensor + Horizon Lock stabilization = Perfection", "CamoGeko-XDA, 30 May 2026Yep, this is one of the reasons the first pixel phones were excellent. 👍🏻Even if they had snapdragon their design, battery siize and charging speeds were crap.", "Zuzuz, 30 May 2026There's a problem with the sensor suppliers. Even if you want 12 huge megapixels/photodio... moreYep, this is one of the reasons the first pixel phones were excellent. 👍🏻", "Honor better have a flat display too this time.", "Fa14bi, 30 May 2026A 1/2.5 Sensor for 100MP sounds like an 500kw car transfering it's power to 3 inch wheels... moreThere's a problem with the sensor suppliers. Even if you want 12 huge megapixels/photodiodes, if nobody makes it, you can't get it.  
I would like to see produced an 1" type sensor in which the size of the pixel is above 2 or 3ym, like in real cameras. Not long ago people had cameras with 5mp, 8mp, 12mp, 16mp, and the quality was and still is excellent.", "A 1/2.5 Sensor for 100MP sounds like an 500kw car transfering it's power to 3 inch wheels.  
 
 
Lots of noise for nothing."]

even worse is the intro is just saying how all smartphones keep chasing these useless features like "brighter screens for HDR" and "having thinner bodies" (reasonable, unless we're talking about phone-to-tablet foldables, which is what the DeltaFold is) and "more pro and more ultra" (also reasonable) and how they're all the same thing at the end of the day (depends, whether you're browsing instagram on a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 phone or editing a 30 minute 4K video on a Helio G81 Phone). and how their phone is different apparently, why? because they said so and because it looks and feels shiny, something people definitely care about the most.", "One year old chip for 6k. Love it fr, keep it coming Ve... morei love when phones are treated like fashion statements more than actual utility devices with fun gimmicks on the side. this company has to be the worst out of all companies i've seen, genuinely why would someone need a Vertu? do they wanna use it as a utility product? literally any phone with a Snapdragon 8 Elite SoC and even iPhones are miles cheaper than it. do they want it as a fashion statement? iPhone Pros exist, you'd probably have a better experience with them than whatever outlandish UX this company makes. 
 
also love the AI images they used for the ads, keep it going vertu: the super duper ultra premium brand, the alpha and the omega.", "I hope the Enigma (Zack JRE) tests it after getting it from another Alpha (MKBHD). will it prove itself to be a true Alpha, or will it be an Omega pretending to be an Alpha...", "Nubia Fold Vertu edition", "Buns company, buns phone, absurd, absolutely atrocious price. 
 
Love it fr, keep it coming Vertu, I like seeing outlandish products"]

It's not about malware scanning. It's about giving potenti... moreSo, “it's about giving potential victims 24 hours to think and consult other people.” Who are those “potential victims” will think in 24 hours, and “consult other people,” when there's already the Play Protect service? 
 
Google Play itself has repeatedly hosted malware, proving that corporate gatekeeping doesn’t guarantee user protection. 
 
And F-Droid said this: 
 
“Furthermore, Google’s framing that they need to mandate developer registration in order to defend against malware is disingenuous because they already have a remediation mechanism for malware they identify on a device: the Play Protect service that is enabled on all Android Certified devices already scans and disables apps that have been identified as malware, regardless of their provenience. Any perceived risks associated with direct app installation can be mitigated through user education, open-source transparency, and existing security measures without imposing exclusionary registration requirements.” 
 
This is like age verification where “it would protect children,” but instead, we got mass surveillance system and a privacy and security risks. 
 
https://f-droid.org/en/2025/09/29/google-developer-registration-decree.html  
 
Google's “solution” is making developers identifiable and controllable. A pathway to internet censorship.

Considering those people's devices are 90% chance infected by all the weird apps in the Google Application Store (aka Play Store) and by all the slop delivered by their unverified developers and their not-properly-vetted apps, and by all the spyware installed by default from factory.

column7:
["Is this sound clear and louder than Mi Bluetooth Speaker?", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreHe didn't test it with any EQ settings mentioned.", "VLAD what you wrote about the bass being practically non-existent is not true. Did you test it with a Xiaomi model cell ?  
I own the Sound Pocket and it rocks whilst playing thru my Poco. Hi-Res is enabled and custom EQ and XEQ apk too. I have gotten many compliments for how it sounds. 😉💪 
Did you test with EQ programmed etc ❓❔🤔", "I would prefer JBL Flip 7 or Sony Ult Field 1 over this even if they cost more than this crap", "The most important points for me were not addressed! What is the battery size? What is the Bluetooth version number? How long does it take to charge? And what is the maximum charging speed?", "GregLu, 31 May 2026With a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle.
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound... moreThe JBL Charge 6 is a beast. Amazing battery life, EQ, it can act as a power bank, and has strong bass. I got one second-hand, and it was a great investment.", "I have jbl go 3. Good small speaker. Battery life is only 5 hours. But build quality is good. And bass is good too. Though I use it very rarely. Mostly listen to music in my car and sometimes on tws earbuds.", "…so when will Xiaomi  sound pause will come out?", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreI don't understand how can people enjoy a strong base. I always turn it way down", "The Xiaomi sound outdoor cost about the same, but way better than this.", "The TWS pair support definitely put it on my list.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreWith a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle. 
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound Outdoor that are 30W and in stereo mode, what a pleasure to use ! I rarely use them inside, but for outside, it's more pleasant than a Charge 6 (not better than 2 Charge 6 though) because of the separation/rendition. 
For the price point, no app (meaning no EQ but also no or less tracking) but a banger, paid 60 € for both, so the price of a Flip 6 in huge promotion ... Better deal, Scamsung can suck it, specially when you see the Xiaomi Sound Party with Harman tuning that seems better than a Charge 6 (I didn't tested the Sound Party to have a honnest opinion about it)", "The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're getting a safe, crowd-pleasing tuning that's suited for most styles of music. 
 
So, weak bass then. 😁", "Yes people really need another "ATLAS" 
Annoying Teenager Loud Audio System.", "looks like a jbl grip level speaker with 30% lower price. good bargain."]

column7:
["NeonHD, 18 hours agoPut a rugged case on and every phone will last forever.I won't fully believe it, especially after my previous phone got broke despite already having a rugged case on it.", "If Xiaomi put USB3 with wired video output support on these two phones, it can easily be a great legit pocket PC since the newer Dimensity chips supports Android 16's Linux Terminal feature, especially 9400 and 9500. It's a real missed opportunity right there.", "NeonHD, 18 hours agoPut a rugged case on and every phone will last forever.Iphone and lasting forever doesn't go together in the same sentence.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Noone buys expensive Xiaomi devices, get a older iPhone for same money that will last forever ... morePut a rugged case on and every phone will last forever.", "This phone configuration only costs about 300 euros in China", "are these pools even worth the time pooling? i mean, the huge mass of human will just grab whatever shiny new things appear. i doubt even for gsm reader, the pool represent a fraction of miniscule readers. also id rather get 15u from last year despite smaller batt (i could easily get cn version) and 8gE", "At €750, ya'll are better off buying last year's Xiaomi 15 Ultra that blows both these phones out of the water.", "Diamond Grape, 31 May 2026Poco software are really bloated while T series aren't. I've used both a poco x3 pro... morePoco X3 Pro is a budget phone. 
Plus all ads can get disabled, it takes 2 minutes to do it from the app menu and you never have to tweak anything else ever.", "The 17T is literally a Poco X8 Pro with the only reason to exist for literally double the price being better cameras. Everything else is exactly the same (optical reader fo 750€? Really?) or worse (15 minute charge rate is 5:21 which is bordering on Sony / Pixel territory and slower than the much memed iPhones, though in Europe where you don't get the special sauce charger in the box they'll probably perform similar) than that phone. It's also in the 'liked by nobody' size where it's too big to be a small (or as close as 6.3 inches gets to 'small') phone and too small to be a big phone at that price point. It's one of those phones that feel like they're made for nobody. For that price difference you could get the X8 Pro (even the Pro Max) and a better camera for what you're saving if you care. 
 
The 17T Pro is somehow even more confusing. The screen there's nothing wrong with, the battery life is very good, charging rate is very strong (9:06 from 15 minutes is a very strong result)... but all of that's also true of the OnePlus 15, which is around the same price, charges even faster (over 10 hours from 15 minutes) and has an ultrasonic fingerprint reader so it might actually work. Only thing going for this is the slightly better main camera. 
 
Overall they both... just exist at this price, the Pro is better but I can't recommend either of them.", "i wouldnt get another xiaomi... not if the software continues to be hyperos", "Price is too high. Can get a proper flagship for that money.", "Diamond Grape, 31 May 2026Poco software are really bloated while T series aren't. I've used both a poco x3 pro... moreoh please they both use hyperos", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Poco x8 pro same phone as 17t but with weaker camera and is more than 50% cheaper Poco software are really bloated while T series aren't. I've used both a poco x3 pro and a 10t so I'm saying this from personal experience", "Xiaomeme phone?? Never and never again going to buy Xiaomeme phone.", "Noone buys expensive Xiaomi devices, get a older iPhone for same money that will last forever and get updates too.", "Carlos Amigo , 31 May 2026Xiaomi 12T pro was the last good 'T-series' phone to release.14T Pro is a banger. Loved it despite preffering small and light phones", "Poco x8 pro same phone as 17t but with weaker camera and is more than 50% cheaper", "ReStore, 31 May 2026sorry, no more Xiaomi phones for me.Why?", "Until June 30 I can buy Xiaomi 17T Pro 12GB/512GB for 799,99 euros or the version 12G/1T for 899.99 euros. I don't know if the 1T version it's worth 100 euros more!", "The "base" 17 if it weren't for not having the Physical Periscope Telephoto.", "both are more than sufficient phones but for those prices I skip. I am using 15t and thinking about giving 15t to my dad and getting 17t or 17t pro to myself but nah i will get another brand for my dad and keep 15t for myself.", "17T is priced 799 in Singapore for 12+256. 
 
 If you consider it has an ultrawide that's not 8MP (and does support 1080p60 on that camera unlike 600 Pro), the periscope lens, the competent Dinensity 8500, and RAM price increases, it's actually a pretty good deal. 
 
17T Pro is 1049 for 12+256 (online-exclusive), and while I'd say that's pretty worth it too given this current trend (and Reno15 Pro Max and Vivo X300 FE), I'd say get the Poco F8 Ultra instead; a more balanced phone overall.", "Not interested in tiny phones.  
THey arent bad but at same price there are countless of much better options.  
For example Motorola Signature here in Slovakia is now 799 eur.", "For this two phones Europe prices are too high. I don't know rest of the world. There are better deals on Poco or Xiaomi or many other brands in Europe.", "sorry, no more Xiaomi phones for me.", "Xiaomi 12T pro was the last good 'T-series' phone to release.", "usb 2.0 in a thousand euro phone is a deal breaker for me. and this was the reason i did not bough the 15t pro, also. i am not that nostalgic about the past millennium, to want to experience again the dial up internet."]

["Glenico353, 6 hours agoTell me, what will happen if the developer doesn't register with Google? You can find the... moreStop posting a link and saying stuff you don't understand. Say what you know or what you think you know in your own words, and maybe I will discuss it with you when I have time.", "...all the malware that normally end up on such devices will find a way to hack through Airdrop's security and steal data or infect that Apple Device. Basically making Apple Devices part of the same terrible experience that Android & Windows users are part of with their infected/malware/spyware ridden experience.", "Anonymous, 15 hours agoYou've got it wrong. 

EU is concerned about interoperability between devices. 

I ha... moreJust from the fact that you wanted to file transfer from an Android or Windows device to your Apple device proves you either don't own an Apple device or at the very most you own one to make you feel a certain way. 
 
True Apple owners know to treat, by default, any and all Android & Windows devices as infected/compromised. Even when they are your own devices. You should never use any real accounts or any of your personal information through such devices. They are just burner devices. 
 
Apple's Airdrop technology is secure. Once Android and (not long after) Windows are given keys to Apple's technology, all the malware", "Anonymous, 13 hours agoI don't know what your point is. Developers don't need to register with Google. User... moreTell me, what will happen if the developer doesn't register with Google? You can find the answer here: "Anonymous, 17 hours agoand what about developer verification genius? stop yapping about advanced flow, everyone knows... moreI don't know what your point is. Developers don't need to register with Google. Users will be able to install unverified apps. It's just that they will have to wait for 24 hours one time.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Still, Android is an inferior operating system to Windows:
I can't manage files as in Wi... moreTry root explorer", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026I, as an Apple user, do not feel at all safe knowing that now, just about any random person ow... moreYou've got it wrong. 
 
EU is concerned about interoperability between devices. 
 
I have both devices and want to be able to send files between them and Android / Windows are doing great without issue. While Apple is great only between Apple devices and not with others, that's the issue. 
 
You can disable who can and cannot see or send something to you. It's just an additional capability not a disability to be able to send between different devices. 
 
Suppose you weren't able to call or send message to those on Android or able to use websites only made by Apple, will that be good? Listen songs on the Apple music only made by Apple themselves, only shows made by Apple and only apply wallpapers made by Apple. Will that be good? Or extremely secure? 
 
If that's the case then you can open your bank account in my bank it is available in all countries. It is world's most secure. There's challenge to everyone if they can break it. But only condition is that you can't deposit any money or anything. But the security is never seen before. I promise no one can steal your money that's guarantee. 
 
Will you open your account with us? Sir? At the minimum cost of $999 only. Keep in mind the security is unbroken since time immemorial.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Misleading and dishonest. It's not about malware scanning. It's about giving potenti... moreand what about developer verification genius? stop yapping about advanced flow, everyone knows about it already. you only believe misinformation propagated by techlore. read the whole thing before spouting bs "Anonymous, 31 May 2026I, as an Apple user, do not feel at all safe knowing that now, just about any random person ow... moreandroid doesn't care about security and privacy either but the difference is you can modify the aosp base to make your own os like grapheneos has and know exactly what is in it unlike ios", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026I, as an Apple user, do not feel at all safe knowing that now, just about any random person ow... moredon't talk about things you know nothing about. free and open source apps are safer than your verified apps with propietary code because anyone can read the source code as opposed to ios's malware ridden apps. neither apple nor the government cares about your security or privacy my gullible friend", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026I, as an Apple user, do not feel at all safe knowing that now, just about any random person ow... moreYou are just upset that the walled garden is getting destroyed. 
Most iOS users are falling prey to simple phising links despite Apple promising 98% protection from phising links.", "Please have an in-built native Apps Lock like what the Chinese phones have had for 10+ years!", "potato4k, 31 May 2026Nobody asked about those 6.6% who are excited for new emojis? 🤭🤪", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Are you dense? Have you seen the millions of malware apps that made it past Google's Play... moreMost online scam victims got scammed by being tricked by scammers to install malware, not because downloading apps from the Google Play Store. Once you installed malware, scammers will be able to transfer all money in your bank account. They don't need to ask you to give them money.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Misleading and dishonest. It's not about malware scanning. It's about giving potenti... moreSo, “it's about giving potential victims 24 hours to think and consult other people.” Who are those “potential victims” will think in 24 hours, and “consult other people,” when there's already the Play Protect service? 
 
Google Play itself has repeatedly hosted malware, proving that corporate gatekeeping doesn’t guarantee user protection. 
 
And F-Droid said this: 
 
“Furthermore, Google’s framing that they need to mandate developer registration in order to defend against malware is disingenuous because they already have a remediation mechanism for malware they identify on a device: the Play Protect service that is enabled on all Android Certified devices already scans and disables apps that have been identified as malware, regardless of their provenience. Any perceived risks associated with direct app installation can be mitigated through user education, open-source transparency, and existing security measures without imposing exclusionary registration requirements.” 
 
This is like age verification where “it would protect children,” but instead, we got mass surveillance system and a privacy and security risks. 
 
 
 
Google's “solution” is making developers identifiable and controllable. A pathway to internet censorship. 
 "Nobody asked about those 6.6% who are excited for new emojis? 🤭", "lets keep it clear 
 
45 % of android users are advanced in app been install from other appstors other than google 
 
but after google looking android ( Which is stupid thing ) this precentage will rise very fast and beleive me 
 
apple got a jailbreak even when they are restricted their OS from the begining ... android will not just have a jailbeark 🤣 it will have demolishedgate because there is, ADB commandline, and you can also disable google play which will distroy google sideloading monitoring program 
like two days ago i tried to install from aurora but google playstore prevent that, so i disabled it then the installation completed without any issue", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026It's for unverified apps. And as I understand it, it's a one-time thing. Seems fair.... moreAre you dense? Have you seen the millions of malware apps that made it past Google's Play Store QC? And no amount of lockdown will protect people from giving money to scammers online who pretend to be family members or friends.", "I, as an Apple user, do not feel at all safe knowing that now, just about any random person owning an android device is going to be able to: 
1) Scan for and discover my Apple devices' Aidrop IDs; 
2) Interact with (ping & transfer information to/from) my Apple devices. 
 
Considering those people's devices are 90% chance infected by all the weird apps in the Google Application Store (aka Play Store) and by all the slop delivered by their unverified developers and their not-properly-vetted apps, and by all the spyware installed by default from factory. 
 
What's next??? Forcing Apple to allow Windows file transfers? It. Just. Makes. No. Sense! 
 
What's the point of building a secure and safe platform only to then be forced to allow all possible security vectors to threaten its existence? Why build great things if you are then forced to just share all their secrets with some companies that were not even able to understand the technology enough to build something similar or at least on the same level as Apple when talking about wireless file transfers? 
 
The EU forcing such things makes it anticompetitive, like it's forcing inventors of technologies to "donate" part of their intellectual property to their competitors so as to help them "catch up" artificially. It's like being the fastest runner, but because you win all the time, the EU is forcing you to share your payout with all those losers who you beat all the time?", "I doubt 🧐 this data is right since I & lot of people are sad about the restricted freedom of installing Apps 😞 the inability to restrict the battery for system Apps", "Meanwhile...in 2025, 239 malware apps were downloaded 42 million times on the playstore.", "Apple wins by doing nothing", "You're going to have polarizing viewpoints on Gemini Intelligence. That will always be the case, as long as we're still in this AI bubble. 
 
Personally, I'm more interested in the phone-to-desktop features, which I hope gets more improvements. That said, I don't want it to outright replace Samsung DeX on Galaxy phone; rather, it should enhance the already powerful DeX experience. We'll see how this goes.", "Glenico353, 31 May 2026Keep Android Open already talks about security:

"The security rationale is a smokescre... moreMisleading and dishonest. It's not about malware scanning. It's about giving potential victims 24 hours to think and consult other people. Yes, malware authors can register with Google, but once it's confirm that their apps are malware, Google can banned them and prevent more installations. Also, if you make malware, will you want to give your ID to Google. 
 
Also, you will be able to install unverified apps. You just have to wait for 24 hours one time. Teenagers can wait for 24 hours. As for companies, it's a given that they will configure company phones to be able to install unverified apps if they will release unverified APKs. And it doesn't make any sense that companies won't register with Google. 
 
Don't believe everything you read on the Internet.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026It's for unverified apps. And as I understand it, it's a one-time thing. Seems fair.... moreKeep Android Open already talks about security: 
 
"The security rationale is a smokescreen. Google Play Protect already scans for malware independent of developer identity. Requiring a government ID doesn't make code safer. It makes developers identifiable and controllable. Malware authors can register. Indie developers and dissidents often can't. The EFF is blunt: identity-based gatekeeping is a censorship tool, not a security one." 
 
This doesn't just hurt users, but independent developers as well: 
 
"A teenager's first app, a volunteer's privacy tool, or a company's confidential internal beta. It doesn't matter. After September 2026, none of these can be installed without Google's blessing."", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Seems like they backed off iirc. And verification was replaced with waiting for 24 hours to in... moreIt's for unverified apps. And as I understand it, it's a one-time thing. Seems fair. Many people got scammed from installing malware.", "Android is full with software and Ai and I really like to see you can choose for just only security and android without all the software like a android light.", "So people are exited about Androslop?", "justasmile, 31 May 2026Not relatedit is related, perhaps reading is outside your forte. it is a part of the extra "security" (read:censorship) features of android 17", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Seems like they backed off iirc. And verification was replaced with waiting for 24 hours to in... moreyou should stick to ios (read everything on this page) "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Seems like they backed off iirc. And verification was replaced with waiting for 24 hours to in... moreyou are conflating developer verification and advanced flow. FOSS app developers have to provide their personal information and pay google to publish apps", "I was "excited" for the emojis 😂", "ProMaxUltraAreBricks, 31 May 2026No way people are excited for the "extra security features" aka make Android sideloa... moreNot related", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Seems like they backed off iirc. And verification was replaced with waiting for 24 hours to in... moreFrom KeepAndroidOpen: 
 
Google says "power users" can "still install" unverified apps. Here's what that actually looks like: 
1. Delve into System Settings, find Developer Options 
2. Tap the build number seven times to enable Developer Mode 
3. Dismiss scare screens about coercion 
4. Enter your PIN 
5. Restart the device 
6. Wait 24 hours 
7. Come back, dismiss more scare screens 
8. Pick "allow temporarily" (7 days) or "allow indefinitely" 
9. Confirm, again, that you understand "the risks" 
 
Nine steps. A mandatory 24-hour cooling-off period. For installing software on a device you own. 
 
Worse: this flow runs entirely through Google Play Services, not the Android OS. Google can change it, tighten it, or kill it at any time, with no OS update required and no consent needed. And as of today, it hasn't shipped in any beta, preview, or canary build. It exists only as a blog post and some mockups.", "The sloppification continues", "ProMaxUltraAreBricks, 31 May 2026No way people are excited for the "extra security features" aka make Android sideloa... moreSeems like they backed off iirc. And verification was replaced with waiting for 24 hours to install non play store APKs. 
 
I still hate this timeline so much.", "Bazinga, 31 May 2026I more prefer stockish interfaceThen oneplus is for you. Avoid pixel or motorola.", "meh .... since its google bloatware flavoured .... better to be ignored", "ProMaxUltraAreBricks, 31 May 2026No way people are excited for the "extra security features" aka make Android sideloa... morewe've lost the fight soldier. not enough people are protesting against developer verification, google does not care either way and the us government won't force companies to abandon anticonsumer practices", "No way people are excited for the "extra security features" aka make Android sideload more complicated and force developers to register with Google 💀 
 
On my Chinese Android phone I already have to waste my time going through a useless package installer and acknowledge nonsense "privacy risk" each time I want to install/update sideloaded apps.", "so many people voted for all those features.. 
 
while I vote none of these interesting features.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026Regular android 17 may be good but one ui 9 will be better skinned version.I more prefer stockish interface", "Still, Android is an inferior operating system to Windows: 
I can't manage files as in Windows. 
I can't open 2 folders at the same time, and copy and paste from one to the other.", "Regular android 17 may be good but one ui 9 will be better skinned version.", "Very excited"]

column7:
["Is this sound clear and louder than Mi Bluetooth Speaker?", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreHe didn't test it with any EQ settings mentioned.", "VLAD what you wrote about the bass being practically non-existent is not true. Did you test it with a Xiaomi model cell ?  
I own the Sound Pocket and it rocks whilst playing thru my Poco. Hi-Res is enabled and custom EQ and XEQ apk too. I have gotten many compliments for how it sounds. 😉💪 
Did you test with EQ programmed etc ❓❔🤔", "I would prefer JBL Flip 7 or Sony Ult Field 1 over this even if they cost more than this crap", "The most important points for me were not addressed! What is the battery size? What is the Bluetooth version number? How long does it take to charge? And what is the maximum charging speed?", "GregLu, 31 May 2026With a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle.
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound... moreThe JBL Charge 6 is a beast. Amazing battery life, EQ, it can act as a power bank, and has strong bass. I got one second-hand, and it was a great investment.", "I have jbl go 3. Good small speaker. Battery life is only 5 hours. But build quality is good. And bass is good too. Though I use it very rarely. Mostly listen to music in my car and sometimes on tws earbuds.", "…so when will Xiaomi  sound pause will come out?", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreI don't understand how can people enjoy a strong base. I always turn it way down", "The Xiaomi sound outdoor cost about the same, but way better than this.", "The TWS pair support definitely put it on my list.", "Anonymous, 31 May 2026The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreWith a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle. 
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound Outdoor that are 30W and in stereo mode, what a pleasure to use ! I rarely use them inside, but for outside, it's more pleasant than a Charge 6 (not better than 2 Charge 6 though) because of the separation/rendition. 
For the price point, no app (meaning no EQ but also no or less tracking) but a banger, paid 60 € for both, so the price of a Flip 6 in huge promotion ... Better deal, Scamsung can suck it, specially when you see the Xiaomi Sound Party with Harman tuning that seems better than a Charge 6 (I didn't tested the Sound Party to have a honnest opinion about it)", "The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're getting a safe, crowd-pleasing tuning that's suited for most styles of music. 
 
So, weak bass then. 😁", "Yes people really need another "ATLAS" 
Annoying Teenager Loud Audio System.", "looks like a jbl grip level speaker with 30% lower price. good bargain."]

column7:
["A55 also, in Romania", "One UI is the gold standard of Android, even better than stock Android, the only drawback is the 'parallel universe' of preinstalled Samsung in-house apps and app store, in addition to Google's standard offerings. Some might consider this as bloat. 
 
Samsung has come a long way since the dark days of Touchwiz. 
 
And definitely better than the Chinese Android skins. They had something potentially good in OxygenOS, but that was killed off and then 'absorbed' into ColorOS."]

["Anonymous, 29 Apr 2013gingerbread had alot of updates, from 2.3.0 to 2.3.7Like I said in the post below, the minor updates GB had do not make major updates. 
 
API bumps are what is considered a major update. Gingerbread had only two major versions, and the first (API 9) was even deprecated, leaving Gingerbread with only one meaningful (i.e., useful, used) version today.", "nmcunix, 28 Apr 2013This would be the first time that an Android version had more than 2 major updates. History sh... moreNo. Jelly Bean had two versions so far. A third version would put it right with HC and EC: 
 
3 versions: 
 
- Honeycomb: 3.0, 3.1 and 3.2 (APIs 11, 12 and 13). 
- Eclair: 2.0, 2.0.1 and 2.1 (APIs 5, 6 and 7). 
 
2 versions: 
 
- Jelly Bean: 4.1.x and 4.2.x (APIs 16 and 17). 
- Ice Cream Sandwich: 4.0-4.0.2 and 4.0.3-4.0.4 (APIs 14 and 15). 
- Gingerbread: 2.3-2.3.2 and 2.3.3-2.3.7 (APIs 9 and 10). 
 
1 version: 
 
- Froyo: 2.2 (API 8). 
- Donut: 1.6 (API 4). 
- Cupcake: 1.5 (API 3). 
 
Jelly Bean would not be the first to achieve 3 versions (i.e., 2 major updates). Eclair was the first to do that, and Honeycomb followed. 
 
Of course... some major versions had lots of minor versions (GB had at least 8 that I know, ICS 5 etc.), but they are bug fixes and backports (case of Open Accessory in 2.3.4) and not make a major version, nor they change the underlying system in meaningful ways to apps (like API bumps do).", "AnonD-135058, 28 Apr 2013Try dolphin browser..

["Anonymous, 29 Apr 2013gingerbread had alot of updates, from 2.3.0 to 2.3.7Like I said in the post below, the minor updates GB had do not make major updates. 
 
API bumps are what is considered a major update. Gingerbread had only two major versions, and the first (API 9) was even deprecated, leaving Gingerbread with only one meaningful (i.e., useful, used) version today.", "nmcunix, 28 Apr 2013This would be the first time that an Android version had more than 2 major updates. History sh... moreNo. Jelly Bean had two versions so far. A third version would put it right with HC and EC: 
 
3 versions: 
 
- Honeycomb: 3.0, 3.1 and 3.2 (APIs 11, 12 and 13). 
- Eclair: 2.0, 2.0.1 and 2.1 (APIs 5, 6 and 7). 
 
2 versions: 
 
- Jelly Bean: 4.1.x and 4.2.x (APIs 16 and 17). 
- Ice Cream Sandwich: 4.0-4.0.2 and 4.0.3-4.0.4 (APIs 14 and 15). 
- Gingerbread: 2.3-2.3.2 and 2.3.3-2.3.7 (APIs 9 and 10). 
 
1 version: 
 
- Froyo: 2.2 (API 8). 
- Donut: 1.6 (API 4). 
- Cupcake: 1.5 (API 3). 
 
Jelly Bean would not be the first to achieve 3 versions (i.e., 2 major updates). Eclair was the first to do that, and Honeycomb followed. 
 
Of course... some major versions had lots of minor versions (GB had at least 8 that I know, ICS 5 etc.), but they are bug fixes and backports (case of Open Accessory in 2.3.4) and not make a major version, nor they change the underlying system in meaningful ways to apps (like API bumps do).", "AnonD-135058, 28 Apr 2013Try dolphin browser..

["Anonymous, 29 Apr 2013gingerbread had alot of updates, from 2.3.0 to 2.3.7Like I said in the post below, the minor updates GB had do not make major updates. 
 
API bumps are what is considered a major update. Gingerbread had only two major versions, and the first (API 9) was even deprecated, leaving Gingerbread with only one meaningful (i.e., useful, used) version today.", "nmcunix, 28 Apr 2013This would be the first time that an Android version had more than 2 major updates. History sh... moreNo. Jelly Bean had two versions so far. A third version would put it right with HC and EC: 
 
3 versions: 
 
- Honeycomb: 3.0, 3.1 and 3.2 (APIs 11, 12 and 13). 
- Eclair: 2.0, 2.0.1 and 2.1 (APIs 5, 6 and 7). 
 
2 versions: 
 
- Jelly Bean: 4.1.x and 4.2.x (APIs 16 and 17). 
- Ice Cream Sandwich: 4.0-4.0.2 and 4.0.3-4.0.4 (APIs 14 and 15). 
- Gingerbread: 2.3-2.3.2 and 2.3.3-2.3.7 (APIs 9 and 10). 
 
1 version: 
 
- Froyo: 2.2 (API 8). 
- Donut: 1.6 (API 4). 
- Cupcake: 1.5 (API 3). 
 
Jelly Bean would not be the first to achieve 3 versions (i.e., 2 major updates). Eclair was the first to do that, and Honeycomb followed. 
 
Of course... some major versions had lots of minor versions (GB had at least 8 that I know, ICS 5 etc.), but they are bug fixes and backports (case of Open Accessory in 2.3.4) and not make a major version, nor they change the underlying system in meaningful ways to apps (like API bumps do).", "AnonD-135058, 28 Apr 2013Try dolphin browser..

column7: ["This is EXACTLY what a tablet should be, big beautiful OLED screen, not a top range chipset, optional pen and keyboard, literally perfect", "What about update support? Don't think it will be good.", "Great, a short-lived unrepairable display to match the short-lived unrepairable battery. The tablet is truly one of the form factors of all time. If you don’t want to sell it for parts after 5 years, get a convertible laptop (but one with a reliable hinge)", "I'm quite surprised other manufacturers still bother with tablets. Apple has 50%, Samsung 30%, Lenovo 10% so there is not much left to fight over! The magnesium chassis is good but the fiberglass (basically fancy plastic) back is not.", "Ah yes Asus, the market definitely needed more generic huge tablets, instead of compact devices like the base Zenfone used to be.", "Anything over $400 isn't competitive. That said, I appreciate ASUS's wise decision to include the D8300, which is barely capable of gaming, rather than having a completely useless SD7s or SD7 series chip."]

column6:
I got into a screaming match on the bus this morning, and honestly, I don't care if the whole vehicle thought I was crazy.

​This guy gets on, sits right across from me, and immediately blasts a video on his phone at full volume. No headphones. Just pure, distorted audio filling the entire space. I tolerated it for five minutes, hoping it was just a quick clip. It wasn’t.

​I politely asked him, Big man, do you mind putting on headphones? It's a bit loud. He looked me dead in the eye and said, If you don’t want to hear it, buy a car. It’s a public space, I can do what I want.

​The absolute entitlement blew my mind. I didn’t even argue. I pulled out my phone, opened an annoying alarm sound, maxed out my volume, and held it right next to his ear. He lost his mind, calling me psyco and aggressive. A few people on the bus told me I should have just ignored him, while others cheered.

​When did public space become an excuse to have zero home training?

 Am I wrong for fighting fire with fire, or are people way too comfortable being completely rude in public?

column6:
Audi B9 S5 launched in 2017 on the MLB Evo platform with a 3.0L turbocharged V6, 354 HP and 500 Nm of torque. 
Lighter than the B8 it replaced, wider track, longer wheelbase, and a larger cabin. 
As a daily driver it is one of the most liveable performance coupes available - refined enough for long motorway journeys, fast at a B-road, and the V6 and torque converter automatic combination is proving to be one of the most reliable setups in any German car. 
The engine is shared across the Porsche Cayenne and Panamera, which tells you how solid it is.

column6:
1.5 years ago, I bought these OnePlus Bluetooth earphones online for around ₹2,500. But I never thought they would turn out like this.

In the beginning, they worked really well. The battery backup was good, and the sound quality was also excellent. But as time passed, problems slowly started appearing.

Now the battery backup has reduced to barely half an hour. Sometimes the earphones suddenly switch off while using them. And the sound quality is no longer the same — the audio has become very poor.

Honestly, companies are looting customers so badly these days. At the time of launch, they promise one thing, but when the product actually reaches users, it turns out to be completely different.

I would never recommend these Bluetooth earphones to anyone.

And before buying any product, always check reviews from genuine verified users first.

column6:
I got into a screaming match on the bus this morning, and honestly, I don't care if the whole vehicle thought I was crazy.

​This guy gets on, sits right across from me, and immediately blasts a video on his phone at full volume. No headphones. Just pure, distorted audio filling the entire space. I tolerated it for five minutes, hoping it was just a quick clip. It wasn’t.

​I politely asked him, Big man, do you mind putting on headphones? It's a bit loud. He looked me dead in the eye and said, If you don’t want to hear it, buy a car. It’s a public space, I can do what I want.

​The absolute entitlement blew my mind. I didn’t even argue. I pulled out my phone, opened an annoying alarm sound, maxed out my volume, and held it right next to his ear. He lost his mind, calling me psyco and aggressive. A few people on the bus told me I should have just ignored him, while others cheered.

​When did public space become an excuse to have zero home training?

 Am I wrong for fighting fire with fire, or are people way too comfortable being completely rude in public?
column146:
☺️Always smiling ☺️.
Distinctive life requires determination ☮️
❤️❤️
Manchester United ♥️

It wasn’t. ​I politely asked him, Big man, do you mind putting on headphones? It's a bit loud. He looked me dead in the eye and said, If you don’t want to hear it, buy a car. It’s a public space, I can do what I want. ​The absolute entitlement blew my mind. I didn’t even argue. I pulled out my phone, opened an annoying alarm sound, maxed out my volume, and held it right next to his ear. He lost his mind, calling me psyco and aggressive. A few people on the bus told me I should have just ignored him, while others cheered. ​When did public space become an excuse to have zero home training? Am I wrong for fighting fire with fire, or are people way too comfortable being completely rude in public?

Audi B9 S5 launched in 2017 on the MLB Evo platform with a 3.0L turbocharged V6, 354 HP and 500 Nm of torque. 
Lighter than the B8 it replaced, wider track, longer wheelbase, and a larger cabin. 
As a daily driver it is one of the most liveable performance coupes available - refined enough for long motorway journeys, fast at a B-road, and the V6 and torque converter automatic combination is proving to be one of the most reliable setups in any German car. 
The engine is shared across the Porsche Cayenne and Panamera, which tells you how solid it is.

column6:
1.5 years ago, I bought these OnePlus Bluetooth earphones online for around ₹2,500. But I never thought they would turn out like this.

In the beginning, they worked really well. The battery backup was good, and the sound quality was also excellent. But as time passed, problems slowly started appearing.

Now the battery backup has reduced to barely half an hour. Sometimes the earphones suddenly switch off while using them. And the sound quality is no longer the same — the audio has become very poor.

Honestly, companies are looting customers so badly these days. At the time of launch, they promise one thing, but when the product actually reaches users, it turns out to be completely different.

I would never recommend these Bluetooth earphones to anyone.

And before buying any product, always check reviews from genuine verified users first.

column6:
I got into a screaming match on the bus this morning, and honestly, I don't care if the whole vehicle thought I was crazy.

​This guy gets on, sits right across from me, and immediately blasts a video on his phone at full volume. No headphones. Just pure, distorted audio filling the entire space. I tolerated it for five minutes, hoping it was just a quick clip. It wasn’t.

​I politely asked him, Big man, do you mind putting on headphones? It's a bit loud. He looked me dead in the eye and said, If you don’t want to hear it, buy a car. It’s a public space, I can do what I want.

​The absolute entitlement blew my mind. I didn’t even argue. I pulled out my phone, opened an annoying alarm sound, maxed out my volume, and held it right next to his ear. He lost his mind, calling me psyco and aggressive. A few people on the bus told me I should have just ignored him, while others cheered.

​When did public space become an excuse to have zero home training?

 Am I wrong for fighting fire with fire, or are people way too comfortable being completely rude in public?

column6:
Audi B9 S5 launched in 2017 on the MLB Evo platform with a 3.0L turbocharged V6, 354 HP and 500 Nm of torque. 
Lighter than the B8 it replaced, wider track, longer wheelbase, and a larger cabin. 
As a daily driver it is one of the most liveable performance coupes available - refined enough for long motorway journeys, fast at a B-road, and the V6 and torque converter automatic combination is proving to be one of the most reliable setups in any German car. 
The engine is shared across the Porsche Cayenne and Panamera, which tells you how solid it is.

No matter your taste, both devices were designed to turn heads. 🔥 The question is which will you personally prefer.

column6:
1.5 years ago, I bought these OnePlus Bluetooth earphones online for around ₹2,500. But I never thought they would turn out like this.

In the beginning, they worked really well. The battery backup was good, and the sound quality was also excellent. But as time passed, problems slowly started appearing.

Now the battery backup has reduced to barely half an hour. Sometimes the earphones suddenly switch off while using them. And the sound quality is no longer the same — the audio has become very poor.

Honestly, companies are looting customers so badly these days. At the time of launch, they promise one thing, but when the product actually reaches users, it turns out to be completely different.

I would never recommend these Bluetooth earphones to anyone.

And before buying any product, always check reviews from genuine verified users first.

column6:
I got into a screaming match on the bus this morning, and honestly, I don't care if the whole vehicle thought I was crazy.

​This guy gets on, sits right across from me, and immediately blasts a video on his phone at full volume. No headphones. Just pure, distorted audio filling the entire space. I tolerated it for five minutes, hoping it was just a quick clip. It wasn’t.

​I politely asked him, Big man, do you mind putting on headphones? It's a bit loud. He looked me dead in the eye and said, If you don’t want to hear it, buy a car. It’s a public space, I can do what I want.

​The absolute entitlement blew my mind. I didn’t even argue. I pulled out my phone, opened an annoying alarm sound, maxed out my volume, and held it right next to his ear. He lost his mind, calling me psyco and aggressive. A few people on the bus told me I should have just ignored him, while others cheered.

​When did public space become an excuse to have zero home training?

 Am I wrong for fighting fire with fire, or are people way too comfortable being completely rude in public?

column6:
Audi B9 S5 launched in 2017 on the MLB Evo platform with a 3.0L turbocharged V6, 354 HP and 500 Nm of torque. 
Lighter than the B8 it replaced, wider track, longer wheelbase, and a larger cabin. 
As a daily driver it is one of the most liveable performance coupes available - refined enough for long motorway journeys, fast at a B-road, and the V6 and torque converter automatic combination is proving to be one of the most reliable setups in any German car. 
The engine is shared across the Porsche Cayenne and Panamera, which tells you how solid it is.

column6:
1.5 years ago, I bought these OnePlus Bluetooth earphones online for around ₹2,500. But I never thought they would turn out like this.

In the beginning, they worked really well. The battery backup was good, and the sound quality was also excellent. But as time passed, problems slowly started appearing.

Now the battery backup has reduced to barely half an hour. Sometimes the earphones suddenly switch off while using them. And the sound quality is no longer the same — the audio has become very poor.

Honestly, companies are looting customers so badly these days. At the time of launch, they promise one thing, but when the product actually reaches users, it turns out to be completely different.

I would never recommend these Bluetooth earphones to anyone.

And before buying any product, always check reviews from genuine verified users first.

column6:
I got into a screaming match on the bus this morning, and honestly, I don't care if the whole vehicle thought I was crazy.

​This guy gets on, sits right across from me, and immediately blasts a video on his phone at full volume. No headphones. Just pure, distorted audio filling the entire space. I tolerated it for five minutes, hoping it was just a quick clip. It wasn’t.

​I politely asked him, Big man, do you mind putting on headphones? It's a bit loud. He looked me dead in the eye and said, If you don’t want to hear it, buy a car. It’s a public space, I can do what I want.

​The absolute entitlement blew my mind. I didn’t even argue. I pulled out my phone, opened an annoying alarm sound, maxed out my volume, and held it right next to his ear. He lost his mind, calling me psyco and aggressive. A few people on the bus told me I should have just ignored him, while others cheered.

​When did public space become an excuse to have zero home training?

 Am I wrong for fighting fire with fire, or are people way too comfortable being completely rude in public?
column146:
☺️Always smiling ☺️.
Distinctive life requires determination ☮️
❤️❤️

It’s a public space, I can do what I want. ​The absolute entitlement blew my mind. I didn’t even argue. I pulled out my phone, opened an annoying alarm sound, maxed out my volume, and held it right next to his ear. He lost his mind, calling me psyco and aggressive. A few people on the bus told me I should have just ignored him, while others cheered. ​When did public space become an excuse to have zero home training? Am I wrong for fighting fire with fire, or are people way too comfortable being completely rude in public?

Audi B9 S5 launched in 2017 on the MLB Evo platform with a 3.0L turbocharged V6, 354 HP and 500 Nm of torque. 
Lighter than the B8 it replaced, wider track, longer wheelbase, and a larger cabin. 
As a daily driver it is one of the most liveable performance coupes available - refined enough for long motorway journeys, fast at a B-road, and the V6 and torque converter automatic combination is proving to be one of the most reliable setups in any German car. 
The engine is shared across the Porsche Cayenne and Panamera, which tells you how solid it is.

column6:
1.5 years ago, I bought these OnePlus Bluetooth earphones online for around ₹2,500. But I never thought they would turn out like this.

In the beginning, they worked really well. The battery backup was good, and the sound quality was also excellent. But as time passed, problems slowly started appearing.

Now the battery backup has reduced to barely half an hour. Sometimes the earphones suddenly switch off while using them. And the sound quality is no longer the same — the audio has become very poor.

Honestly, companies are looting customers so badly these days. At the time of launch, they promise one thing, but when the product actually reaches users, it turns out to be completely different.

I would never recommend these Bluetooth earphones to anyone.

And before buying any product, always check reviews from genuine verified users first.

column6:
I bought a pair of high-end, studio-grade noise-canceling headphones to help me focus while working from my apartment. The isolation was incredible; when I turned on the active noise cancellation (ANC), the entire world went completely dead silent.
Last night, I was wearing them while washing dishes, facing the kitchen wall.
At 11:40 PM, the headphones gave a low dual-tone chime, the signal that the battery was at 1%.
A second later, they died, and the ANC cut out.
The sudden rush of ambient apartment sounds returned. But it wasn't the sound of the refrigerator hum or the traffic outside.
It was the sound of a heavy, metallic zipper being slowly pulled down, followed by a wet, raspy sigh.
Directly into my right ear.
I froze, the soapy plate slipping from my hands and shattering in the sink. The noise hadn't come from behind me.
The headphones hadn't been blocking out the sounds of the apartment. They had been blocking out the sound of whatever had been standing chest-to-back with me for the last three hours.

...

column6:
Day 35 of building Jodu - a photo collage maker for iPhone 

I got some pretty feedback on Reddit! They were so nice there I couldn’t believe it was Reddit. Many were blown away by the quality of the app, many android requests and finally one wanted to use the app to make App Store screenshots! 

It’s true that it’s your users who find unique uses for your products.

I fixed a bunch of issues mainly with dragging images across the carousel canvas.
Wrote up a new tutorial blogpost. 
Still waiting for my latest version to get reviewed.
column24:
Day 34 of building Jodu - a photo collage maker for iPhone 

Looks like the Reddit outreach and blog creation is somewhat paying off. Got really diverse downloads today.

Not much dev work in the weekend so I just go on reddit threads and DMs for cold outreach and test the app as much as I can.

column6:
Nigerians really don't appreciate how cheap gadget repairs are here.

A friend of mine abroad literally waits until someone is coming to Nigeria just to send his faulty phones for repairs.

He was billed $120 to fix an iPhone 14 Pro Max charging port in the US. I got it done for him here for ₦30k.

The tech arbitrage is real. 📱🇳🇬

I finally got the courage to transfer my main sim. Starting today, I will be using this Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra as my main phone/daily driver. 

Brings back a lot of memories from when I had my first Android phone 😂 Gosh why am I getting emotional. 

But yeah, I hope I don’t miss my iPhone so much, especially the size of the Pro. It is still with me, but I’m taking the S24 Ultra with me most of the time. 😉
